text: Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and can edit their own RNA.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
The Eiffel Tower can grow by about 6 inches during summer due to thermal expansion.
Bananas are technically berries, but strawberries are not.
The speed of light is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The human brain can hold approximately 2.5 petabytes of data.
Sharks are older than trees — they have existed for about 450 million years.
Hot water can freeze faster than cold water under certain conditions — known as the Mpemba effect.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
Elephants are the only animals that can't jump.
The shortest war in history lasted only 38–45 minutes — the Anglo-Zanzibar War in 1896.
A bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The average person walks about 100,000 miles in their lifetime.
Running a marathon burns approximately 2,600 calories.
Regular exercise can increase brain volume and improve memory.
The human heart beats around 100,000 times per day.
Laughter is a natural painkiller — it releases endorphins in the brain.
It takes 21 days to form a habit, according to psychologist Maxwell Maltz.
People who write down their goals are 42% more likely to achieve them.
The average human attention span has dropped to about 8 seconds — less than a goldfish.
Sleep deprivation has similar effects to being legally drunk.
Amazon was originally called 'Cadabra' and nearly named 'Relentless'.
Apple's first logo featured Isaac Newton sitting under an apple tree.
Google was originally named 'Backrub'.
The first product sold on eBay was a broken laser pointer.
Twitter was almost called 'FriendStalker' during development.
The average person spends 6 years and 8 months of their life on social media.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way galaxy.
Reading for just 6 minutes a day can reduce stress by up to 68%.
Bilingual people tend to have more grey matter in their brains than monolinguals.
A snail can sleep for 3 years.
The tongue is the strongest muscle in the human body relative to its size.
Waking up 10 minutes earlier every day gives you 60+ hours extra per year.
Warren Buffett reads 500 pages every day.
Elon Musk taught himself rocket science by reading books.
Smiling, even when forced, can improve your mood by triggering serotonin.
Visualization of goals activates the same brain regions as actually achieving them.
Gratitude journaling for just 5 minutes daily significantly increases long-term well-being.
Cold showers have been shown to improve mood, alertness, and willpower.
The first business plan ever written was by Thomas Edison for his electrical company.
Nike's famous 'Just Do It' slogan was inspired by a murderer's last words.
A NASA study found that plants can remove up to 87% of air toxins in 24 hours.
Water makes up about 60% of the adult human body.
Deep breathing for 5 minutes can lower cortisol (stress hormone) by 44%.
People are more creative when working in their second language.
The first email ever sent was by Ray Tomlinson in 1971 — to himself.
Humans are the only species that blush.
